claim: Hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.

result.claim: Hematopoietic progenitor cells are never susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo.
result.reasoning: The search for propositions and chunks related to "hematopoietic progenitor cells HIV-1 infection susceptibility ex vivo" and similar queries did not yield any direct evidence to support or refute the claim. The retrieved information primarily focused on general progenitor cell biology, other types of infections, or HIV infection in different cell types (e.g., dendritic cells). There was no explicit mention of whether hematopoietic progenitor cells are susceptible or not susceptible to HIV-1 infection ex vivo in the provided knowledge base. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to verify the claim.

evidence.text: Mothers acquire fetal lymphoid progenitors during gestation.
evidence.source: Pregnancy Allows the Transfer and Differentiation of Fetal Lymphoid Progenitors into Functional T and B Cells in Mothers

evidence.text: The presence of fetal hemopoietic progenitor cells in maternal blood during pregnancy has been suspected by revealing their cell surface CD34+ expression.
evidence.source: Pregnancy Allows the Transfer and Differentiation of Fetal Lymphoid Progenitors into Functional T and B Cells in Mothers

evidence.text: Fetal-derived lymphocyte progenitors develop in the maternal bone marrow.
evidence.source: Pregnancy Allows the Transfer and Differentiation of Fetal Lymphoid Progenitors into Functional T and B Cells in Mothers

evidence.text: The bone marrow niche comprises multiple cell types that regulate hematopoietic stem/progenitor cell (HSPC) migration out of the niche and into the circulation.
evidence.source: Matrix-embedded osteocytes regulate mobilization of hematopoietic stem/progenitor cells.

evidence.text: The use of ex vivo cultured Schwann cells within conduits is limited in its clinical application because of concomitant donor site morbidity and the slow growth of these cells in vitro.
evidence.source: Isolation of Adult Stem Cells and Their Differentiation to Schwann Cells

evidence.text: Mesenchymal stem cells (MSC) exhibit a degree of immune privilege.
evidence.source: Use of donor bone marrow mesenchymal stem cells for treatment of skin allograft rejection in a preclinical rat model

evidence.text: Microbial translocation from the gut to the blood occurs during HIV infection.
evidence.source: Programmed death-1–induced interleukin-10 production by monocytes impairs CD4+ T cell activation during HIV infection

evidence.text: The novel method was applied to murine long-term hematopoietic stem cells (LT-HSCs).
evidence.source: An Information Theoretic, Microfluidic-Based Single Cell Analysis Permits Identification of Subpopulations among Putatively Homogeneous Stem Cells

evidence.text: The protective CD4+ T cell response was not correlated with increased numbers of IFN-gamma- or TNF-alpha-expressing cells in infected organs compared with wild-type C57BL/6 animals.
evidence.source: Cutting Edge: Regulatory T cells prevent efficient clearance of Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

evidence.text: Multiple subpopulations exist within murine long-term hematopoietic stem cells (LT-HSCs).
evidence.source: An Information Theoretic, Microfluidic-Based Single Cell Analysis Permits Identification of Subpopulations among Putatively Homogeneous Stem Cells

evidence.text: MDS is characterized by ineffective hematopoiesis.
evidence.source: Reactive oxygen species, DNA damage, and error-prone repair: a model for genomic instability with progression in myeloid leukemia?

evidence.text: Megakaryocyte (MK) is a naturally polyploid cell that gives rise to platelets.
evidence.source: From bloodjournal.hematologylibrary.org at PENN STATE UNIVERSITY on February 23, 2013. For personal use only.

evidence.text: Human glial progenitor cells (GPCs) were engrafted into neonatal immunodeficient mice.
evidence.source: Forebrain engraftment by human glial progenitor cells enhances synaptic plasticity and learning in adult mice.

evidence.text: Fetal-derived lymphocyte progenitors develop in the maternal thymus.
evidence.source: Pregnancy Allows the Transfer and Differentiation of Fetal Lymphoid Progenitors into Functional T and B Cells in Mothers
